id: De twee gezichten van Agentic AI wapen en schild__c5-sentence-window__0006
text:
The world of cyber security is at a tipping point. Agentic AI — artificial intelligence in which systems operate autonomously and make decisions without human intervention — is fundamentally changing how both defenders and attackers work. Unlike generative AI, which depends on pre-defined instructions, these “agents” can initiate, execute and improve actions on their own based on what they learn. Recent research by Palo Alto Networks, a US cyber security company, shows how profound this shift is. Researchers managed to simulate a full ransomware attack in just 25 minutes using Agentic AI — a hundred times faster than traditional methods. “The scenario they outline is disturbingly realistic,” says Matthijs Mons, cyber security specialist at ABN AMRO. “Criminals will develop agents that are tailored to targeted attacks.”
From science fiction to reality.

So far, Agentic AI is mainly a leap in efficiency. “The major cyber security companies are investing heavily in AI,” says Mons. “But criminals are just as active. The romantic image of hackers as lonely boys in basements is completely outdated.
